title: Emit streaming events on post create, delete, edit
description:
After successful post creation, emit streaming events:
- Public/PublicLocal channels: emit 'update' event if visibility=public
- User channels: emit 'update' event for each local follower of the author
- Hashtag channels: emit 'update' event on each Hashtag(tag) channel matching tags in the post
- Direct channel: emit 'update' (actually 'conversation') for direct posts to local actors

On post delete: emit 'delete' event with status ID on all channels that received the original 'update'.
On post edit: emit 'status.update' event.

AGENT WORKFLOW: Spawn a research subagent to design the fanout architecture: should we collect affected channels synchronously before returning, or use a separate fanout task? Recommend the simpler approach.

TEST DISCIPLINE:
- Create public post → verify 'update' event received on Public channel within 100ms
- Create post with #rust → verify 'update' on Hashtag('rust') channel
- Delete post → verify 'delete' event on correct channels
- Edit post → verify 'status.update' event
- Post from account with no followers → no User channel events emitted (no crash)

DEFENSIVE PROGRAMMING:
- Fanout must not block the HTTP response — use tokio::spawn for fanout
- Fanout panic must not crash the server — wrap in catch_unwind equivalent
- Don't emit events for non-public posts on public channels
acceptance_criteria: Public post → update on Public channel; hashtag post → update on Hashtag channel; delete → delete event; edit → status.update; fanout doesn't block HTTP response
close_reason: Streaming events already wired in posting.rs
